title: uputames
text: uputames Put an asynchronous message in the input queue of the calling application. Explain this code short uputames (unsigned char*message, longlength); Copy Parameters message Message data to send length Size of the message data Note: The maximum length of a message is 255 bytes. Return Values 0— uputames always returns zero. Use Library: yrtl Description uputames places an asynchronous message, containing the specified data, in the input queue of the calling application. If the calling application has code in the receiveMessage trigger, either in the application shell or in the active form, that trigger will be activated, and the data is passed to it in $result , as soon as the application is idle, that is, when it is not reading user input or processing ProcScript. Because it can never be guaranteed that the application will process the message, we refrain from using the term ‘sending a message’. All this routine does is put the message in a queue. If the application has no code in a receiveMessage trigger anywhere, the message will never be seen. Uniface removes any non-printable characters (ASCII value less than 32 or greater than 127) before placing the received message in $result . This is done because these characters have special meaning in Uniface, and are not generally usable, or even desirable, in ProcScript. A message containing non-printable characters should be processed in 3GL code. For this purpose, the service routine ugetames provides the message in its original, unfiltered format. uputames cannot be used to send an asynchronous message to another application. Example: uputames The following example sends data in a C structure. Because of the way the structure members are filled, using strcpy , the structure contains non-printable characters (the zero terminators of the strings). However, these are not transmitted to $result , as the resulting output shows. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <string.h> EXPORT void send_code_and_description () { struct { char code[6]; char desc[6]; } data; strcpy(data.code, "123"); strcpy(data.desc, "Desc"); (void)uputames((unsigned char *)&data, sizeof(data)); } Copy The receiveMessage trigger contains this code: Explain this code message/info "ASYNC> Got async message:[%%$result]" Copy The output from the receiveMessage trigger is: Explain this code ASYNC> Got async message:[123Desc Copy Related Links ugetames Get the complete, unfiltered asynchronous message from the input queue.
